executionValidation: Every Git code_input uses profileId git, fixedLanguage bash, recipeType shell_task, and mode terminal_workspace.
Repository outcomes use gitExpectations as the primary proof of completion.
Ordinary file-tree outcomes may use workspaceExpectations, and exact safe command evidence may use terminalExpectations.
Independent exercises use per-exercise workspaces; progressive projects and capstones use project-scoped workspaces.
Every terminal exercise prompt tells the learner to type each command and press Enter.
repositoryValidation: Never grade exact commit hashes, environment-dependent dates, absolute paths, or decorative log formatting.
Set a deterministic local identity and main branch when commits or branch names matter.
Use local bare repositories for remote workflows and forbid public network dependencies.
Validate final files, branches, commit counts/messages, ignored paths, remote branches, and clean state from repository state.
progression: Git Foundations contains no branches, merges, remotes, or GitHub collaboration.
Git Branches, Merges, and Recovery assumes the local commit workflow and contains no hosted collaboration dependency.
GitHub Collaboration assumes branch, merge, conflict, and recovery skills and does not re-teach them as standalone topics.
pedagogy: Open each course with a story-led standalone introduction, not an internal curriculum checklist.
Every standard module has exactly one cumulative module project.
Every final module has exactly one capstone section and one capstone topic.
Project and capstone step counts come exactly from projectBrief.stepCountTarget.
